hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular representations is applied to the search space of hypothetical linear polyamines, then it will accelerate the discovery of high-performance CO2 capture materials by efficiently identifying top-performing candidates within a limited simulation budget compared to random or exhaustive screening approaches, because the surrogate models can learn structure-property relationships from sparse labeled data and guide sampling toward promising chemical regions.
